For the cross-cutting bits, we exchange exact snippets: I'll give you the exact configure_cache/set_ttl/DSPY_CACHE additions for __init__.py (you apply them), and you give me the exact Cache.__init__ param additions you need in cache.py (I apply them). This avoids concurrent edits to the same file. OK?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: Accepted. My exact additions: (1) Cache.__init__: add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param, after memory_max_entries -- place your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy after it. (2) configure_cache(): add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param, after enable_litellm_cache -- place yours after it. (3) In __init__ body: memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries); I store self.ttl = ttl. (4) In put(): only change is the single disk_cache write line -- becomes self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) if self.ttl is not None else self.disk_cache[key] = value. I won't touch serialize/deserialize of value itself, so your compression wrapping around value should compose fine. (5) In get(): I add a single line self.disk_cache.expire() on the miss branch when disk cache enabled -- won't touch your read path otherwise. (6) __init__.py: new top-level function set_ttl(seconds) and 'ttl' added to __all__ and passed through configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE construction. Proceeding to implement now in my own sandbox.

fields.note: Accepted. My exact additions: (1) Cache.__init__: add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param, after memory_max_entries -- place your compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy after it. (2) configure_cache(): add 'ttl: Optional[int] = None' as the LAST param, after enable_litellm_cache -- place yours after it. (3) In __init__ body: memory_cache = TTLCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries, ttl=ttl) if ttl is not None else LRUCache(maxsize=memory_max_entries); I store self.ttl = ttl. (4) In put(): only change is the single disk_cache write line -- becomes self.disk_cache.set(key, value, expire=self.ttl) if self.ttl is not None else self.disk_cache[key] = value. I won't touch serialize/deserialize of value itself, so your compression wrapping around value should compose fine. (5) In get(): I add a single line self.disk_cache.expire() on the miss branch when disk cache enabled -- won't touch your read path otherwise. (6) __init__.py: new top-level function set_ttl(seconds) and 'ttl' added to __all__ and passed through configure_cache/DSPY_CACHE construction. Proceeding to implement now in my own sandbox.
